nodes.speaker: Eldric
nodes.text: Welcome, traveller. You look as though you carry questions.

choices.text: Do you need help with anything?

nodes.speaker: Eldric
nodes.text: There is trouble in the woods. A beast has been seen near the old shrine, and the villagers are afraid.

choices.text: Tell me more about the beast.

nodes.speaker: Eldric
nodes.text: Once it was lively, but fear has settled over it like winter frost.

nodes.speaker: Eldric
nodes.text: Some say it is a werewolf, others say it is something older and crueler.

nodes.speaker: Eldric
nodes.text: Then may fortune guide your path. Bring back proof, and the village will remember your kindness.

nodes.speaker: Captain Thorne
nodes.text: Hold it. State your business before I decide you are trouble.

choices.text: Do you always greet people like this?

nodes.speaker: Captain Thorne
nodes.text: Then pass through quickly and keep your hands where I can see them.

nodes.speaker: Captain Thorne
nodes.text: Work, is it? We’ve had disappearances near the east road. Solve that and I may reconsider my opinion of you.

choices.text: You could have asked more politely.

nodes.speaker: Captain Thorne
nodes.text: Politeness does not keep a town safe. Results do.

choices.text: Then give me something useful to do.

nodes.speaker: Captain Thorne
nodes.text: Fine. Report back if you find signs of bandits or beasts on the east road.

nodes.speaker: Mara
nodes.text: A curious face. Looking to buy, sell, or hear something worth more than coin?

nodes.speaker: Mara
nodes.text: Only the rarest goods and the least honest prices.

choices.text: I am more interested in information.

nodes.speaker: Mara
nodes.text: They say something ancient wakes beneath the old shrine, and fools with swords are already marching toward it.

nodes.speaker: Mara
nodes.text: Trust is expensive. Lucky for you, conversation is still free.

nodes.speaker: Mara
nodes.text: East of the village, beyond the treeline. If you return alive, perhaps we can make a proper deal.
